regions.name: Bend left
regions.note: Slow down and steer gently to the left. Keep left of the road centre. Watch for oncoming vehicles cutting the corner.

regions.name: Bend right
regions.note: Slow down and steer gently to the right. Stay in your lane. Watch for oncoming vehicles and pedestrians around the bend.

regions.name: Successive bends to the left
regions.note: Reduce speed before entering the bends. Do not overtake. Stay well within your lane through each curve.

regions.name: Successive bends to the right
regions.note: Reduce speed before entering the bends. Do not overtake. Stay well within your lane through each curve.

regions.name: Steep descent ahead
regions.note: Engage a lower gear before descending. Avoid riding the brakes continuously. Maintain control and keep distance from the vehicle ahead.

regions.name: Steep ascent ahead
regions.note: Engage a lower gear to maintain power. Be prepared for slow-moving vehicles ahead. Do not overtake on the climb unless you have a clear view.

regions.name: Road narrows ahead
regions.note: Reduce speed. Be prepared to give way to oncoming traffic. Do not attempt to overtake where the road is narrow.

regions.name: Road narrows from right
regions.note: The road narrows on the right side. Keep left and slow down. Give way to oncoming traffic if necessary.

regions.name: Road narrows from left
regions.note: The road narrows on the left side. Keep right and reduce speed. Watch for pedestrians or obstacles on the left.

regions.name: River bank or quayside
regions.note: Slow down significantly. Do not park near the edge. Watch for the road ending abruptly near a bank or water edge.

regions.name: Uneven road surface ahead
regions.note: Reduce speed to avoid loss of control. Hold the steering wheel firmly. Motorcyclists should be especially cautious.

regions.name: Severe bump ahead
regions.note: Slow down well before the bump. Crossing at speed can damage the vehicle and cause loss of control.

regions.name: Severe dip ahead
regions.note: Reduce speed. The vehicle may bottom out or become airborne if taken too fast. Watch for water pooling in the dip.

regions.name: River bend crossing (drift) ahead
regions.note: Slow down and assess water depth before crossing. Do not cross if water is fast-flowing or above safe level.

regions.name: Slippery road
regions.note: Reduce speed immediately. Avoid sudden braking, accelerating or sharp steering. Increase following distance.

regions.name: Loose surface road
regions.note: Reduce speed. Loose gravel can cause skidding. Increase following distance to avoid stone-throw damage.

regions.name: Danger of falling and fallen rocks ahead
regions.note: Drive with caution. Watch for rocks on or near the road. Do not stop or park under rocky slopes.

regions.name: Pedestrian crossing
regions.note: Slow down and be prepared to stop. Give way to pedestrians already on or approaching the crossing.

regions.name: Children crossing ahead
regions.note: Reduce speed significantly. Be especially alert. Children may cross unexpectedly, so prepare to stop at any moment.

regions.name: Cyclists
regions.note: Watch for cyclists on or joining the road. Give at least 1.5 metres clearance when overtaking.

regions.name: Cattle crossing ahead
regions.note: Slow down and watch for animals on the road. Stop if necessary and wait for them to clear.

regions.name: Wild animals
regions.note: Reduce speed and scan road edges for animals. Do not hoot aggressively, as it may cause animals to panic into your path.

regions.name: Road works ahead
regions.note: Slow down and follow temporary signs or flagmen. Expect lane changes, uneven surfaces, and workers near the road.

regions.name: Traffic light signals ahead
regions.note: Prepare to stop. Reduce speed and be ready to obey the traffic light. Do not speed up to beat the lights.

regions.name: Low-flying aircraft crossing ahead
regions.note: No driving action required. Be aware of aircraft at very low altitude nearby (usually near airstrips).

regions.name: Two-way traffic ahead
regions.note: You are entering a two-way road. Keep left and watch for oncoming vehicles. Do not straddle the centre line.

regions.name: Hazard ahead
regions.note: An unspecified danger is ahead. Reduce speed, be alert, and proceed with caution.

regions.name: Road junction ahead
regions.note: A junction is approaching. Check mirrors, reduce speed, and watch for vehicles entering from side roads.

regions.name: Junction ahead: stop or give way
regions.note: Prepare to stop or give way at the approaching junction. Act according to whichever sign is present.

regions.name: Junction ahead with road entering from right
regions.note: A road joins from the right. Watch for merging vehicles. Be prepared to adjust speed or position.

regions.name: Junction ahead with acceleration lane from right
regions.note: Vehicles may be joining from an acceleration lane on the right. Maintain steady speed to allow safe merging.

regions.name: Roundabout ahead
regions.note: Reduce speed. Give way to traffic already circulating in the roundabout. Indicate before exiting.

regions.name: Rail crossing with barrier
regions.note: Stop when barriers are down or lights flash. Do not cross until barriers are fully raised and it is safe.

regions.name: Rail crossing without barrier
regions.note: Slow down, look both ways, and listen for trains before crossing. Never race a train to the crossing.

regions.name: Stop
regions.note: You MUST come to a complete stop at the stop line. Look both ways and proceed only when the road is completely clear. Slowing down is NOT enough; you must fully stop.

regions.name: Give way
regions.note: Slow down and yield to traffic on the major road. You do not have to stop if the road is clear, but must always give priority to others.

regions.name: Direction to be followed
regions.note: You must travel in the direction indicated by the arrow. No other direction is permitted at this point.

regions.name: Vehicles travel straight
regions.note: All vehicles must go straight ahead. Turning left or right is not permitted here.

regions.name: Vehicles turn right junction
regions.note: All vehicles must turn right. Straight ahead or left turns are not allowed at this junction.

regions.name: Vehicles travel straight or right
regions.note: Vehicles may only go straight or turn right. Left turns are prohibited at this junction.

regions.name: Pass to the left
regions.note: You must pass the obstacle or island on your left side. Do not go to the right of this sign or island.

regions.name: Compulsory pedestrian foot path
regions.note: Pedestrians must use this path. Cyclists and motor vehicles are not allowed on the pedestrian path.

regions.name: Compulsory track for cattle
regions.note: Livestock must use the designated track. Drivers should slow down, as animals may be present in this area.

regions.name: Compulsory minimum speed (cattle area)
regions.note: A minimum speed applies. Do not drive below the indicated speed unless road conditions demand it.

regions.name: Compulsory minimum speed
regions.note: You must drive at least the speed shown. Driving below this speed is prohibited (common on highways and expressways).

regions.name: End of compulsory minimum speed
regions.note: The minimum speed requirement ends here. Normal speed limits apply from this point.

regions.name: No entry
regions.note: Entry is completely prohibited. Do not enter this road or area. Often used for one-way streets or restricted zones.

regions.name: Road closed
regions.note: The road is closed to all traffic. Turn around and find an alternative route. Do not bypass barriers.

regions.name: Entry forbidden to all vehicles
regions.note: No vehicle of any type is allowed beyond this point, motorised or non-motorised.

regions.name: Entry forbidden to all motor cycles
regions.note: Motor cycles are not allowed beyond this point. Motorcyclists must seek an alternative route.

regions.name: Entry forbidden to all bicycles
regions.note: Bicycles are prohibited beyond this point. Cyclists must dismount and seek an alternative route.

regions.name: No entry for pedestrians
regions.note: Pedestrians are not allowed in this area or on this road. Use an alternative pedestrian route or crossing.

regions.name: No entry for animal drawn vehicles
regions.note: Carts, wagons, or any animal-pulled vehicles are not permitted. Find an alternative route.

regions.name: No entry to all hand carts
regions.note: Hand-pushed carts, trolleys, or barrows are not allowed beyond this point.

regions.name: No entry for farm vehicles
regions.note: Agricultural vehicles such as tractors are prohibited. Use designated agricultural routes.

regions.name: No entry for heavy commercial vehicles
regions.note: Trucks, lorries, and heavy goods vehicles are not permitted. Use alternative designated routes.

regions.name: No entry for power driven vehicles
regions.note: All motorised vehicles are prohibited. Only non-motorised transport may be allowed.

regions.name: No entry for power driven or animal vehicles
regions.note: Neither motorised nor animal-drawn vehicles are permitted. Restricted to pedestrians or emergency access only.

regions.name: No entry for vehicles exceeding width shown
regions.note: If your vehicle is wider than the measurement shown, you cannot proceed. Seek an alternative route.

regions.name: No entry for vehicles exceeding height shown
regions.note: If your vehicle exceeds the height shown, do not proceed. There is a risk of hitting an overhead structure such as a bridge.

regions.name: No entry for vehicles exceeding weight shown on one axle
regions.note: If any single axle exceeds the weight shown, do not proceed. Overloading can damage road surfaces and bridges.

regions.name: No entry for vehicles exceeding weight shown
regions.note: If your vehicle's total weight exceeds the limit shown, you may not proceed. Common near bridges with load restrictions.

regions.name: No entry for vehicles exceeding length shown
regions.note: If your vehicle (including trailer) exceeds the length shown, you cannot proceed. Find a route for long vehicles.

regions.name: No entry: vehicles must keep minimum distance shown
regions.note: You must maintain at least the distance shown between your vehicle and the one ahead. Common in tunnels and on bridges.

regions.name: No left turn
regions.note: Turning left at this junction is prohibited. You must go straight or turn right as permitted.

regions.name: No right turn
regions.note: Turning right at this junction is prohibited. You must go straight or turn left as permitted.

regions.name: No U-turn
regions.note: Making a U-turn is prohibited at this location. Find a safe and legal place further ahead to turn around.

regions.name: Overtaking of four-wheeled vehicles prohibited
regions.note: You may not overtake cars or other four-wheeled vehicles here. Typically placed where visibility or road width makes it dangerous.

regions.name: Overtaking of goods vehicles prohibited
regions.note: You may not overtake trucks or lorries in this section. Remain behind goods vehicles until the prohibition ends.

regions.name: Speed limit
regions.note: Do not exceed the speed shown (in km/h). Maintain this speed or below until you see an end-of-limit sign.

regions.name: Sounding of horns or sirens prohibited
regions.note: Do not use your horn or any audible warning device in this zone. Common near hospitals, schools, or residential areas.

regions.name: Stop at control post
regions.note: You must stop at the control point (checkpoint, toll gate, border post). Await clearance before proceeding.

regions.name: Stop: children school
regions.note: You must stop when children are crossing. Used near school entrances with a crossing patrol or crossing zone.

regions.name: Priority for on-coming traffic
regions.note: Oncoming vehicles have priority on this narrow section. Wait until the road is clear before proceeding through.

regions.name: End of all prohibitions
regions.note: All previous prohibitions imposed on moving vehicles are cancelled from this point. Normal rules apply.

regions.name: End of speed limit
regions.note: The speed limit in force is now lifted. Normal speed limits for the road type apply, unless another sign indicates otherwise.

regions.name: Parking prohibited
regions.note: You may not park here. You may stop briefly to drop off or pick up passengers but must not leave the vehicle unattended.

regions.name: Parking or waiting prohibited
regions.note: Neither parking nor momentarily stopping is allowed here. Keep moving and find a legal stopping place elsewhere.

regions.name: No stopping zone
regions.note: Stopping is completely prohibited throughout the marked zone. Do not stop for any reason, including dropping passengers.

regions.name: Additional panel (direction)
regions.note: Supplementary sign placed below a main sign to clarify direction (e.g. an arrow pointing left or right). Follow the direction indicated.

regions.name: Additional panel (distance ahead)
regions.note: Supplementary sign indicating the specific distance ahead at which the restriction or hazard applies.

regions.name: Additional panel (extra detail)
regions.note: Supplementary sign providing extra detail about the main sign above, such as time of day or category of vehicle affected.

regions.name: Additional panel (road length)
regions.note: Shows the length of road over which a restriction or hazard applies (e.g. '15m' means the restriction applies for the next 15 metres).
